source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Gold-standard electrophysiology in behaving rats demonstrating that hippocampal place-cell sequences generated before goal-directed navigation depict future goal-directed trajectories. The sequences are goal-biased (current location to known goal) and predict immediate future behaviour even for novel start-goal combinations. This is direct evidence for the hippocampus generating prospective rollouts indexed by a destination (goal) coordinate. The main caveat for ARC-018 is that the indexing dimension here is spatial location rather than the E2 action-object coordinate posited in the V3 framing; the biology supports the rollout mechanism but is agnostic about what the coordinate system encodes at a computational level.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, rat hippocampus generates brief sequences encoding spatial trajectories strongly biased to progress from the subject's current location to a known goal location; these sequences predict immediate future behaviour even for novel start-goal combinations.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-018 proposes that the hippocampus generates explicit rollouts indexed by E2 action-object coordinates and updated by E3 harm/goal error to form a viability map. Pfeiffer & Foster demonstrate the generative rollout mechanism itself: hippocampus produces prospective sequences anchored to a goal coordinate at the moment deliberation is required. In the V3 framing, the 'goal location' corresponds to an attractor in E2 action-object space; the forward sweep to that attractor is the rollout ARC-018 describes.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study uses spatial location as the coordinate system. ARC-018 V3 requires the coordinate to be E2 action-object, not merely place. The data are silent on whether the same mechanism generalises to abstract action-object coordinates -- this is the key open question for V3 validation.
mapping.source_context: Rodent (Long-Evans rat), open arena, reward-based navigation task, single-unit recording CA1.

confidence_components.notes: Highest quality evidence for the rollout mechanism. Transfer risk is moderate: spatial indexing is established but generalisation to abstract action-object indexing requires V3 experiments. The goal-biased nature of the sequences is a very strong match to the viability-map framing.
